title: Ternstroemia dubia
intro: Ternstroemia dubia is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Ternstroemiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ternstroemia dubia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, with some preparations also being used in traditional folk remedies for wound healing and skin care.
taxonomy: Ternstroemia dubia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ternstroemiaceae. The plant belongs to the order Ericales and the family Ternstroemiaceae is a part of the clade Asterids. Ternstroemia dubia is further classified under the genus Ternstroemia and is a part of the family Ternstroemiaceae. The family Ternstroemiaceae consists of 7-10 genera with around 100 species. Ternstroemia dubia is native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia.
